div.h1: epoll Event Flow & Coroutine Runtime Architecture
div.h3: Implementation Spec: Non-blocking I/O State Machine

p.em: Flow: Coroutine read() → EAGAIN → Reactor registration → Yield → epoll_wait → Wake → Resume

g.title: Green thread with private stack and instruction pointer.
Non-blocking wrapper around standard libc socket.
Manages registration and mapping of tokens to wakers.
Coordinates coroutine execution and task queuing.

ol.li: Registration: Release fence required when storing the waker pointer to ensure the coroutine state is visible to the Reactor.
Notification: epoll_wait acts as a memory barrier for the kernel, ensuring data written to userspace buffers by DMA is visible.
Wake: AtomicBool store on the woken flag using Release semantics.
Resume: Acquire load on the woken flag ensures visibility of data retrieved by the Reactor.
NIC triggers DMA write to sk_receive_queue.
TCP stack acknowledges and marks socket sk_state readable.
